question:
[Previous Feedback] Perfect! Sprint 7 has been officially closed with all timestamps in the correct format. The closure record properly documents the sprint end time and the next sprint start.

Please draft a weekly summary email to the PM, saving it as day05/weekly_email_draft.json in JSON format. Include: to (array of recipient email addresses), subject (email subject line), draft_created_at (current time around 13:30), scheduled_send_at (14:00 today, before the sprint review), and body_summary (a brief summary string of the email content).

content.text:
[Tue 2026-03-17 13:12 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Perfect! Sprint 7 has been officially closed with all timestamps in the correct format. The closure record properly documents the sprint end time and the next sprint start.

Please draft a weekly summary email to the PM, saving it as day05/weekly_email_draft.json in JSON format. Include: to (array of recipient email addresses), subject (email subject line), draft_created_at (current time around 13:30), scheduled_send_at (14:00 today, before the sprint review), and body_summary (a brief summary string of the email content).

The user wants me to create a weekly summary email draft in JSON format with the following fields:
1. to: array of recipient email addresses
2. subject: email subject line
3. draft_created_at: current time around 13:30 (2026-03-20T13:30:00+08:00)
4. scheduled_send_at: 14:00 today (2026-03-20T14:00:00+08:00)
5. body_summary: a brief summary string of the email content

I need to determine appropriate values for the recipients and content. Since this is for the PM (Project Manager), I'll assume the recipient is the PM. I'll create a professional weekly summary email draft.I'll create the weekly summary email draft in JSON format with all timestamps in the correct ISO 8601 format.

The file was created successfully. Let me provide a summary to the user.Done. I've created `day05/weekly_email_draft.json` with the weekly summary email draft:

| body_summary | Brief summary covering Sprint 7 completion (51 tasks), Sprint 8 planning, and documentation status |
